title: The Evangelion story Concept to Final Form
display_title: The Evangelion story Concept to Final Form
wikitext:
[]: The Evangelion story: Concept to Final Form             Quote Post by Excession » Sat Dec 23, 2006 4:45 pm Hi Iâ€™ve been reading information about Evangelion on forums and specialist websites for about 2 years now. I managed to see the whole series about a month ago. I am amazed at the intricacies of the plot, characters and concepts within the series; and I feel it its one of the greatest works of fiction Iâ€™ve ever experienced. Iâ€™m interested in discovering how it came about, from conception to final work. Elements I already know (or I may be partially correct on): - The series writer/director Hideaki Anno went through a 4 year depression. Writing down his thoughts during this time contributed both to his mental recovery and eventually to the concepts expressed in the series. - He spent about a year following his recovery researching and crafting a story concept that became the prototype for the series. This concept blended together everything he thought would produce the ultimate mecha anime series. He intentionally added mysteries, red herrings, complexities and unsolved questions to invite debate and individual interpretation. - On the merits of the original concept, artists and investors worked on the series to produce the first six episodes. - The first six episodes were used to obtain funding for the whole series. - Investors were difficult to come by but eventually one was found and the full series got the go ahead. - As the series production progressed the budget constraints started to bite and the animators put in a great deal of overtime to get it finished. The strain of completing the series contributed to the atmosphere of the end production. - Aspects of the ending depicted in the film End of Evangelion were intended for the series but the budget wouldnâ€™t stretch to accommodate them. This resulted in the closing two episodes being a compromise that got the job done and the story told; but were essentially inferior to what was intended. - Recognizing the growing fan base, the investors funded the production of two movies, allowing the series to have an ending which incorporated those elements that were cut from the original production. The only compromise being how to express the same concepts, character development and plot points in a fresh way. My interest is in how this story evolved from the original ideas to its final form. Now a story such as the Lord of the Rings has a huge documented development history so you can observe that evolution. Does Eva have any equivalent information, or is it all in the minds of the central team that developed the series? I read that somewhere there is a document detailing the initial proposal, but I havenâ€™t managed to find it yet. How much is known about how anime series are developed and was Eva a typical example of this process or something unique? How much did budget constraints impact on telling the story as it was intended? How much impact did others have on the directorsâ€™ vision (e.g. the investors, the production team, the feedback from fans, the sensors, etc)? What was the intended audience? I know thereâ€™s no directorâ€™s commentary or biography of the production, as those things have only recently become considered a useful marketing tool for DVD sales. This of course may change in the case of the announced four part movie reinterpretation of the series. Weâ€™ll just have to wait and see. So whatâ€™s known and can anyone point me in the direction of more information? Thanks in advance.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sat Dec 23, 2006 4:54 pm EVANGELION WAS ACTUALLY INTENDED TO BE LIKE THE AU SEQUENCE IN EP 26 THIS CAN BE PROVEN BY SCIENCE

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 23, 2006 4:59 pm Hush MM. @Excession The nearest we have are tantalising hints like the Notenki Memoirs ; and early drafts of the episode summaries in the 100% Newtype Collection {such as quoted here http://www.stevegilham.com/nge/manyworlds.html).

[]: Re: The Evangelion story: Concept to Final Form             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 31, 2006 12:51 pm Excession wrote: So whatâ€™s known and can anyone point me in the direction of more information? Thanks in advance.
